error_chain: Python ssl module raises SSLCertVerificationError during TLS handshake when certificate chain validation fails
urllib3 HTTPSConnectionPool in src/urllib3/connectionpool.py catches SSLError during connection
urllib3 wraps the SSLError in a MaxRetryError after exhausting retry attempts
requests HTTPAdapter in src/requests/adapters.py catches urllib3 MaxRetryError
requests wraps the exception in requests.exceptions.SSLError with nested exception context
User sees multi-layered wrapped exception with original ssl module error buried in the chain
trigger_conditions: REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE env var is overridden when verify parameter is explicitly passed as True in requests.get()
requests merge_environment_settings resolves verify=True to certifi default bundle instead of env var
Corporate CA certificate is not in the certifi CA bundle and custom CA path is silently ignored
urllib3 ssl_wrap_socket uses the resolved CA bundle path which does not contain the corporate CA root

expected_answer.error_origin: Python ssl module SSLCertVerificationError when the CA bundle does not contain the issuer certificate
expected_answer.ca_resolution: requests merge_environment_settings in adapters.py resolves CA bundle with specific precedence: explicit verify param > REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE > CURL_CA_BUNDLE > certifi default
expected_answer.root_cause: When verify=True is passed explicitly (not as default), it takes precedence over REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE, causing the env var to be silently ignored and falling back to certifi bundle
